subject: Physics
title: Faraday's Big Discovery
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Faraday's Big Discovery.

hook.text: In 1831, Michael Faraday proved that a changing magnetic field creates electricity—unifying two forces.

buildup.text: Faraday wrapped wire around an iron ring. When he connected a battery to one coil, the other coil produced a momentary current.

discovery.text: It wasn't the magnet that created current—it was the change. A static magnet does nothing; a moving one generates power.

twist.text: Faraday had almost no formal education. He started as a bookbinder's apprentice and taught himself science.

climax.text: His discovery of electromagnetic induction is the basis of every generator, transformer, and power plant on Earth.

punchline.text: Move a magnet near wire and electricity flows. Faraday powered the future.

quiz.question: What did Faraday discover about magnetism and electricity?
options: A changing magnetic field induces an electric current
Static magnets produce a constant current
Electricity and magnetism are unrelated forces
